title: Refund and additional payment of income tax
text:
Refund and additional payment of income tax

Due date for the refund and additional payment of income tax is 1 October 2026.

Refund of income tax

If a tax return was submitted through the e-services environment e-MTA and the information declared does not require subsequent verification, the overpaid amount of income tax would be returned since 5 March 2026.

You can see the income tax return decision in the e-services environment e-MTA by clicking on the heading "Decisions" on the income tax return page.

We will refund the overpaid amount of income tax to the bank account indicated in the tax return. If the bank account number is not pre-filled in the 2025 income tax return or you want to change the pre-filled bank account number, you must confirm the change with your PIN2 code. This requirement is established as an additional security measure.

If you are unable to sign the addition or change of bank account number on your income tax return, please send us a message in the e-services environment e-MTA. Please include the following information in your message:

your name and personal identification code;

screenshots of the error message (including the web browser address bar and the time the image was taken);

a detailed description of the problem and the actions you took before the error occurred;

which authentication tool (ID-card, mobile-ID, Smart-ID) you tried to sign with.

From 18 March 2026 the Estonian Tax and Customs Board started refunding income tax to the taxpayers who submitted their income tax returns on paper.

The Estonian Tax and Customs Board will refund the overpaid amounts of income tax to the bank accounts indicated on the tax returns not later than by 1 October 2026.

Income tax can be refunded if income has been received in Estonia and the payer (employer, the Social Insurance Board, etc.) has withheld income tax on the income.

On the basis of correct tax returns that have passed the provisional checking, the Estonian Tax and Customs Board would issue continuously the payment orders to the banks, in the order generated by the computer system after completing the checking. Tens of thousands of payment orders are issued at the same time and are arranged according to the banks. Exact date of the transfer of an amount to a person's bank account depends also on a bank's operation. It may happen that the persons who submitted their tax returns on the same date are refunded on different dates and it may even occur that a tax return submitted later may be refunded earlier.

Income tax is refunded to the bank account entered in the income tax return. The overpaid income tax can be left in the prepayment account for future tax liabilities.

NB! Any overdue liabilities (incl. enforcement agent's claims) are paid from the refundable income tax first , and then the remaining overpaid income tax is transferred to the bank account. The order of payment of obligations with the same due date is based on the order specified in subsection 6 of § 105 of the Taxation Act.

Additional income tax due

Dates

Due date for payment of a natural person's additional income tax for 2025 is 1 October 2026.

Sole proprietors are also required to pay the additional income tax, social tax and mandatory funded pension by 1 October 2026.

Tax notice

Each person who submitted the income tax return on paper and has to pay additional income tax will receive a tax notice from the Estonian Tax and Customs Board by post.

Tax notices will not be issued to those who have submitted the income tax return electronically; the calculation details of the amount of tax subject to payment additionally are available in the e-services environment e-MTA.

Payment

The most convenient way to pay the tax is electronically in the e-services environment e-MTA via the link on the information page of the income tax return.

The tax can also be paid in the e-MTA, selecting Accounting – Payment, as well as by bank transfer and in the service bureaus using debit or credit card. More information: Payment of taxes.

private client

Last updated: 31.03.2026
